human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for tour-operations-specific boundary discipline, manifest and ticket readiness, guide and transport dependency handling, refund and policy exception escalation, fraud and safety stop conditions, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

review_notes: All required scenario families for the issue and retail-consumer-and-hospitality research contract are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for execution-processing-and-fulfillment plus retail-consumer-and-hospitality are represented and validated.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to day-of-tour execution readiness, exception handling, lifecycle progression, and completion evidence without booking, pricing, refund, supplier-substitution, fraud, safety, permit, or legal authority drift.

Authority sources cover NPS commercial-use authorization, FTC fee and travel-fraud guidance, DOT public charter refund boundaries, FMCSA bus safety search, PCI payment-security handling, ISO adventure-tourism safety management, and ASTA complaint evidence handling.

Scenario set covers missing artifacts, booking and ticket mismatches, policy exceptions, guide rework, transport safety, fraud signals, high-volume disruptions, completion evidence gaps, conflicting policy, tour terminology, and tenant fact adaptation.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; jurisdictional, permit, transport, supplier, venue, processor, and tenant differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
